# Smart Farming: Sustainable Agricultural Storage, Irrigation, and Greenhouse Systems

![](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/2025/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Thrive-logo-icon-colour.png)

[Energy](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/energy/), [Environment](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/environment/), [Innovation](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/innovation/), [Sustainability](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/sustainability/)

## Introduction to Smart Farming

According to recent estimates, the world population [will hit 9.6 billion by 2050](https://un.org/development/desa/en/news/population/world-population-prospects-2019.html), which will require a 70% increase in food production. This presents a major challenge for farmers who already struggle with limited resources, [climate change](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/the-effects-between-climate-change-and-biodiversity/), and a growing demand for sustainable farming practices ([Elkady, 2023](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/ai-smart-farming-future-agriculture-sami-elkady-/)). The agricultural sector must adopt the [Internet of Things](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/smart-farming-future-of-agriculture/) (IoT), which involves using [sensors](https://www.iot-now.com/2024/10/30/147619-how-iot-is-transforming-yields-and-optimising-resources-in-agriculture/#:~:text=Agricutlure%20IoT%20involves%20using%20sensors,can%20monitor%20and%20use%20remotely.), connected equipment, [software](https://www.iot-now.com/2024/10/30/147619-how-iot-is-transforming-yields-and-optimising-resources-in-agriculture/#:~:text=Agricutlure%20IoT%20involves%20using%20sensors,can%20monitor%20and%20use%20remotely.) and data science to continuously [track and regulate](https://www.iot-now.com/2024/10/30/147619-how-iot-is-transforming-yields-and-optimising-resources-in-agriculture/#:~:text=Agricutlure%20IoT%20involves%20using%20sensors,can%20monitor%20and%20use%20remotely.) the farming processes. This adoption is essential to overcoming environmental challenges and ensuring sufficient food for the growing population ([TEKSUN, n.d](https://teksun.com/blog/smart-agriculture-top-applications-of-iot-in-agriculture/)). [Smart farming](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/smart-farming-future-of-agriculture/) is a high-tech, efficient method of cultivation and food production that is environmentally friendly. It is a method of integrating smart electronics and cutting-edge [technology](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/can-we-use-artificial-intelligence-for-sustainability/) into [agriculture](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/ways-to-end-poverty-in-the-world/). Smart farming is heavily reliant on the IoT, eliminating the necessity for manual labor by farmers and growers and enhancing productivity across various dimensions ([TEKSUN, n.d](https://teksun.com/blog/smart-agriculture-top-applications-of-iot-in-agriculture/)).

![](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/2025/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/071624.RSS_.Scalable-Technological-S.webp)

## Definition

### Smart Farming

[Smart farming](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/smart-farming-future-of-agriculture/) involves the application of modern Information and Communication Technologies (ICT) into agriculture, which benefits large conventional farms, small family farming, and organic farms. It also minimises the ecological footprint of farming. [Smart farm](https://rpsaus.com.au/blog/what-is-smart-farming/) is revolutionising the way farming is done. IoT solutions allow farmers to save time, reduce waste, and increase productivity by connecting their farms and providing them with [remote control and monitoring abilities](https://rpsaus.com.au/services/control-monitoring-design/).  
  
[Smart farming](https://www.slideshare.net/slideshow/climate-smart-farming-strategies-and-challenges/275350420) is a modern farming approach that uses technology to boost productivity, streamline labor, cut costs, increase profits, and reduce environmental impact.

![](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/2025/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/1-1.png)

### Sustainable Agricultural Storage

In today’s agricultural industry, efficient storage and inventory management are essential for profitability ([Ain, 2025](https://www.hashmicro.com/my/blog/agriculture-warehouse/)). Sustainable storage isn’t just a fad—it’s a pressing necessity. Similar to adopting a plant-based diet to combat environmental degradation and choosing between a gas-guzzler and an electric car. No longer are storage solutions solely about brick and mortar. Modern farm and agricultural buildings have evolved to incorporate sustainable practices, ensuring functionality and minimal environmental impact ([Rooks, 2024](https://greenerideal.com/guides/sustainable-eco-friendly-farm-equipment-storage-solutions-for-the-modern-farmer/)). Sustainable storage practices aim to minimise the negative environmental impacts associated with conventional storage, including resource depletion, waste generation, and carbon emissions. The use of [renewable energy](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/breakeven-renewable-energy/) sources, for instance, can significantly reduce the [carbon footprint](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/6-ways-to-reduce-our-carbon-footprint/) of storage facilities. Furthermore, the selection of materials with low embodied energy and the implementation of effective [waste management](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/waste-for-energy-the-power-of-biogas-and-biomass/) strategies can minimise the environmental burden of storage ([Sustainability Directory, 2025](https://sustainability-directory.com/term/eco-friendly-storage/)).

![](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/2025/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/image4-1024x684.jpg)

The modern farm’s most valuable allies for storage are:

#### Grain Drums

Grain drums are strong containers made from steel or recycled plastic. They protect grains from moisture, pests, and bad weather. These drums are eco-friendly because they use recycled materials, helping farmers store grains safely while caring for the [environment](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/environmental-pollution-are-we-on-course-to-stop-it/). Their durability and versatility make them a great choice for keeping grains fresher for longer ([Rooks, 2024](https://greenerideal.com/guides/sustainable-eco-friendly-farm-equipment-storage-solutions-for-the-modern-farmer/)).

#### Root Cellars

Root cellars are underground storage spaces that use the earth’s natural insulation to keep food fresh. They maintain a steady temperature, making them perfect for storing root vegetables, fruits, and fermented products. This traditional, energy-free method is an eco-friendly way to preserve food, proving that nature’s solutions are often the best ([Rooks, 2024](https://greenerideal.com/guides/sustainable-eco-friendly-farm-equipment-storage-solutions-for-the-modern-farmer/) & [Kaspari, 2020](https://www.uaf.edu/ces/publications/database/food/vegetable-storage.php)).

![](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/2025/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Best-30-DIY-Root-Cellar-Plans-Ideas-for-Year-Round-Freshness-1.jpg)

#### Hay Barns

The [optimal storage](https://agriculture.vic.gov.au/about/media-centre/media-releases/2024-releases/storing-hay-on-farm#:~:text=The%20optimal%20storage%20for%20hay,due%20to%20the%20weight%20density.) for hay is under cover with protection from the weather on the side of the most common wind direction. Placing a tarp under the haystack can help prevent [moisture spoilage](https://agriculture.vic.gov.au/about/media-centre/media-releases/2024-releases/storing-hay-on-farm#:~:text=The%20optimal%20storage%20for%20hay,due%20to%20the%20weight%20density.) on the bottom bales. Square bales are the most efficient to store under cover and to transport, due to the [weight density](https://agriculture.vic.gov.au/about/media-centre/media-releases/2024-releases/storing-hay-on-farm#:~:text=The%20optimal%20storage%20for%20hay,due%20to%20the%20weight%20density.). [Hay storage](http://chrome-extension://efaidnbmnnnibpcajpcglclefindmkaj/https://www.mla.com.au/globalassets/mla-corporate/research-and-development/program-areas/feeding-finishing-and-nutrition/feedlot-design-manual/033-hay-storage-and-processing-2016_04_01.pdf) planning is essential to ensure year-round availability, as hay is typically harvested [annually](http://chrome-extension://efaidnbmnnnibpcajpcglclefindmkaj/https://www.mla.com.au/globalassets/mla-corporate/research-and-development/program-areas/feeding-finishing-and-nutrition/feedlot-design-manual/033-hay-storage-and-processing-2016_04_01.pdf), often during grain harvest.

The required storage volume depends on feeding needs, purchasing timing, bale type, and [available space](http://chrome-extension://efaidnbmnnnibpcajpcglclefindmkaj/https://www.mla.com.au/globalassets/mla-corporate/research-and-development/program-areas/feeding-finishing-and-nutrition/feedlot-design-manual/033-hay-storage-and-processing-2016_04_01.pdf). Covered storage preserves hay quality for over 12 months, while uncovered outdoor storage leads to nutrient loss within six months. Storage facilities should be designed based on bale size, with adequate clearance for handling equipment, typically requiring a [minimum roof height](http://chrome-extension://efaidnbmnnnibpcajpcglclefindmkaj/https://www.mla.com.au/globalassets/mla-corporate/research-and-development/program-areas/feeding-finishing-and-nutrition/feedlot-design-manual/033-hay-storage-and-processing-2016_04_01.pdf) of 5.4 meters. Compressed hay bales offer a space-saving and efficient storage solution while simplifying [transportation](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/low-carbon-transport-can-electric-vehicles-carry-freight/). Using biodegradable wrapping instead of plastic makes hay an eco-friendly choice. This innovation supports sustainable farming by reducing waste and promoting easier, greener hay transport ([Rooks, 2024](https://greenerideal.com/guides/sustainable-eco-friendly-farm-equipment-storage-solutions-for-the-modern-farmer/)).

![](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/2025/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/5.png)

### Sustainable Irrigation

[Water for agriculture](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/water-for-sustainable-agriculture-and-food-security/) is in short supply and this is worsening in numerous regions globally. Overdraft of groundwater supplies [jeopardises future irrigation capacity](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9177853/), while overdraft of surface waters disturbs important riparian zones, which are the [lands](https://www.waternsw.com.au/education/keeping-waterways-healthy/water-on-your-property#:~:text=Riparian%20zones,and%20can%20be%20easily%20degraded.) next to creeks, rivers, and wetlands. In the context of water resources, “[overdraft](https://www.ppic.org/blog/learning-the-language-of-groundwater/#:~:text=Overdraft:%20When%20groundwater%20extraction%20exceeds,pumping%20water%20from%20deeper%20depths.)” means extracting groundwater faster than it can be replenished by natural processes like rainfall and runoff. Seemingly, [water quality problems](https://en.unesco.org/waterquality-iiwq/wq-challenge) such as salinisation, nutrient overload, and pesticide pollution are very common. [Agricultural management techniques](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/sustainable-water-management-future-generations/) will need to be significantly improved, where the sustainable use of irrigation water is a priority for agriculture, especially in arid areas. Efficiently utilising [reduced-volume irrigation methods](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/water-for-sustainable-agriculture-and-food-security/), choosing and developing more salt and drought-tolerant crop species, and managing soils and crops to minimise water loss are all strategies to use water more effectively in sustainable agroecosystems.

Low Volume Irrigation.  
Source: [IFAS,2012](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQYU5mmYxsA)

From the farmer’s viewpoint, sustainable irrigation means having a steady, uninterrupted water source at a price that doesn’t exceed the marginal revenue they aim to make. From the environmental viewpoint, it means only using volumes of water that can be naturally replaced within a limited timeframe that doesn’t run the risk of endangering future requirements ([Ware, 2023](https://www.twl-irrigation.com/sustainable-irrigation/)).

![](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/2025/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Irrigation-Field-1.jpg)

Drip irrigation is the [most water-efficient irrigation system](https://www.agrivi.com/blog/drip-irrigation-as-the-most-efficient-irrigation-system-type/), capable of dramatically reducing a farm’s water use while increasing crop yields and quality ([AGRIVI, n.d](https://www.agrivi.com/blog/drip-irrigation-as-the-most-efficient-irrigation-system-type/)). The two main types of more sustainable irrigation systems in use today are drip and sprinkler irrigation. The decision of which of the two types to use is dependent on various factors, including:

![](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/2025/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Drip-Irrigation-as-the-Most-Efficient-Irrigation-System-Type-1200x565-1.jpg)

### Sustainable Greenhouse Systems

Greenhouse gardening is an efficient way to grow food on a relatively small piece of property without compromising [soil](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/soil-chemistry-and-sustainability/) quality. Greenhouses help keep surrounding areas undisturbed, preserving the [ecosystems](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/ecosystems-interconnectedness-living-things/) and wildlife present ([Green Business Benchmark, 2021](https://www.greenbusinessbenchmark.com/archive/6-step-greenhouse-guide)). Sustainable greenhouse systems use eco-friendly materials, energy-efficient glazing, and other techniques to reduce energy use and [greenhouse gas](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/greenhouse-gases-is-it-just-a-lot-of-hot-air/) emissions ([Agritech tomorrow, 2024](https://www.agritechtomorrow.com/article/2024/11/6-steps-to-design-a-sustainable-greenhouse/16083#:~:text=When%20building%20an%20eco%2Dfriendly,reliance%20on%20external%20energy%20sources.)). Sustainable greenhouse horticulture can be achieved by employing different cultivation techniques, adequate equipment management, and innovative materials aimed to reduce agro-chemicals, energy use, [water consumption](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/sustainability-for-water-consumption-are-we-prepared/), and waste generation ([Vox et al., 2010](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Giuliano-Vox/publication/285928780_Sustainable_greenhouse_systems/links/577cc2e508ae3f37c5d53514/Sustainable-greenhouse-systems.pdf?origin=publication_detail&_tp=eyJjb250ZXh0Ijp7ImZpcnN0UGFnZSI6InB1YmxpY2F0aW9uRG93bmxvYWQiLCJwYWdlIjoicHVibGljYXRpb25Eb3dubG9hZCIsInByZXZpb3VzUGFnZSI6InB1YmxpY2F0aW9uIn19&__cf_chl_tk=tk.n.wG9pCz46tVhn0pm.45sj8Jd69JzAkjB5L31WFM-1741520590-1.0.1.1-z.MD1Rdfq6YS11czBoptz1UBsrB4Vvbj1IxrSeeJZKA)). ## Smart Farming Technologies

Smart farming technologies have the potential to revolutionise agriculture by enhancing resource efficiency, productivity, and [sustainability](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/the-steady-state-economy-limits-to-economic-growth/) ([Brenes & Marin-Raventos, 2024](https://cacm.acm.org/latin-america-regional-special-section/scalable-technological-architecture-empowers-small-scale-smart-farming-solutions/)). The [Internet of Things (IoT)](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/smart-farming-future-of-agriculture/), Big Data, Artificial Intelligence (AI), geo-positioning systems, and drones are some of the technologies applied to agriculture, in combination with ICT. Following a cycle of Observation – Diagnostics – Decision Making – Action, these modern technologies aim to increase the [quantity and quality of production](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/smart-farming-future-of-agriculture/) as well as the optimisation in terms of the required human labour. Smart farming uses technology to improve agricultural practices, including crop monitoring, livestock management, and [water management](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/sustainable-water-management-the-future-of-recycled-water/) ([Gill, 2024](https://www.itsallgoodsinc.com/insights/smart-farming-transforming-agriculture-with-cutting-edge-technology#:~:text=Livestock%20Monitoring.%20Livestock%20monitoring%2C%20also%20known%20as,more%20accurately%20and%20efficiently%20than%20ever%20before.)).

![](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/2025/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2-1.png)

Advanced technologies powering today’s smart farming include:

### Information and Communications Technology (ICT):

Facilitates data capture, storage, processing, security, and transfer, enabling efficient organisation and management of agricultural data.

### Internet of Things (IoT):

Utilises sensor-equipped devices to monitor crops, livestock, and equipment, while UAVs (Unmanned Aerial Vehicles), commonly referred to as drones, are revolutionising smart farming by enabling tasks like crop health monitoring, precision spraying, field mapping, and irrigation management, leading to more efficient and sustainable agriculture practices.

### Artificial Intelligence (AI) & Machine Learning (ML):

Analyses big data from IoT, providing predictive analytics, weather forecasting, and optimised decision-making for crop production, land use, and supply chains.

### Automation & Robotics:

Boosts efficiency with autonomous tractors, robotic seeding, harvesting, and UAV-assisted precision application of fertilisers and pesticides, reducing environmental impact.

## Case Study

[Sundrop Farms](https://www.sundropfarms.com/our-facilities/) operates under a unique concept where there is only one season—the growing season. By leveraging advanced agricultural technology, the farm maintains optimal conditions for plant growth, ensuring the production of high-quality fruits and vegetables throughout the year. Located in Port Augusta, South Australia, Sundrop Farms has established a world-class [20-hectare greenhouse](https://www.sundropfarms.com/our-facilities/) that is now fully operational. The foundation for this large-scale facility was laid in 2009 with the construction of a 0.2-hectare pilot site. This initial project played a crucial role in refining and developing the innovative technology that underpins Sundrop Farms’ sustainable farming approach, which now extends across more than 200,000 square meters.

[Sundrop Farms](https://www.sundropfarms.com/our-technology/) is a pioneering agricultural enterprise that integrates [sustainable practices](https://www.sundropfarms.com/our-technology/) in storage, irrigation, and greenhouse management. Operating in arid conditions, the company developed innovative methods to grow crops using minimal finite resources.

![](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/2025/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2-2.png)

[Tome City in Miyagi](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/smart-farming-future-of-agriculture/), a major rice-growing region in Japan, was facing issues as a result of an ageing population and a lack of labour force. In an effort to curb these challenges, farmers have introduced drones to maintain production. As a result, the farmers are reportedly using significantly less water and need less labour, while optimising their spraying times.

## Conclusion and Call to Action (CTA)

As the global population grows, so do the challenges owing to limited agricultural land and resources. To address this, the agricultural industry is turning to [internet connectivity solutions](https://www.kaaiot.com/iot-knowledge-base/smart-farming-with-iot-integration#:~:text=be%20highly%20efficient.-,Conclusion,precision%2C%20efficiency%2C%20and%20sustainability.) like IoT to reduce reliance on manual labor. IoT in smart farming helps optimise productivity by using sensors to monitor crop fields for light, humidity, temperature, soil moisture, and crop health. This allows farmers to [remotely manage](https://www.kaaiot.com/iot-knowledge-base/smart-farming-with-iot-integration#:~:text=be%20highly%20efficient.-,Conclusion,precision%2C%20efficiency%2C%20and%20sustainability.) irrigation and other tasks, improving efficiency and minimising waste.

In summary, smart farming, powered by IoT integration, leads the charge in [agricultural innovation](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/advanced-farming-profits-from-innovation/), providing precision, efficiency, and sustainability. The progression to smart cloud farming enhances these advantages, utilising advanced technologies to optimise resource use, improve data analytics, and bolster resilience amid evolving agricultural environments. This comprehensive approach tackles current challenges and charts a course for a connected, data-driven future, fostering more efficient, productive, and eco-conscious agriculture. Smart farming, coupled with smart cloud solutions, signifies a transformative path toward a technologically advanced and resilient agricultural sector ([Gran, 2025](https://www.kaaiot.com/iot-knowledge-base/smart-farming-with-iot-integration#:~:text=be%20highly%20efficient.-,Conclusion,precision%2C%20efficiency%2C%20and%20sustainability.)).

## Achieving THRIVE project goals & The UN’s SDGs

Smart farming, through sustainable agricultural storage, irrigation, and greenhouse systems, aligns with [THRIVE’s 12 Foundational Focus Factors](https://strive2thrive.earth/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/TP-Brochure.pdf) (FFFs), particularly [Values-Based Innovation](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/thrive-framework-values-based-innovation/), [Finite Resources](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/thrive-framework-finite-resources/), and [Science-Based Targets](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/2024/thrive-framework-science-based-targets/).

### Values-Based Innovation:

Related to strategies are the vehicles by which the quest for [sustainability](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/2024/home-sustainability-how-you-can-reduce-global-warming/), and by extension, [thrivability](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/thrive-framework-values-based-innovation/) are enabled. The requirement for these shared values is essential on multiple levels.

### Finite Resources:

Resources that we consume faster than they can be replaced are called non-renewables. As non-renewable resources are finite resources in nature, the sole option is to minimise depletion through reduction, reuse, and [recycling](https://thrivabilitymatters.org/recycling-human-waste-sustainable-sewage/))

### Science-Based Targets:

Science-based targets guide entities in contributing to the common goal of reducing greenhouse gas emissions. They also provide a clear framework and methodologies.

Smart farming enhances productivity, sustainability, and efficiency while reducing costs and environmental impact through advanced technology. # Reasons Steel Barns are the Smart Choice for Agribusiness Investment

![Reasons Steel Barns are the Smart Choice for Agribusiness Investment](https://www.vikingbarns.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/reasons-steel-barns-are-the-smart-choice-for-agribusiness-investment.webp)

Weatherproof agricultural buildings are a must for storing farm equipment and mountains of hay and grain. But is this the only benefit farmers and ranchers consider when buying a building? No. They also need many other benefits to ensure the structure serves them best and fulfills their agricultural needs.

Metal barn buildings offer several advantages that effectively meet the needs of modern farmers. These large clear-span structures offer shelters that feel comfortable year-round to horses, cattle, hens, grain, crops, and more. So, if you feel agribusiness is intimidating, try investing in steel barns.

You have the right to know everything about how these structures help agriculture businesses. We have shared all the details below that will help you decide whether to install barn structures on your field.

## Top Reasons Farmers Choose Steel Barns Nowadays

According to the USDA, agricultural businesses lose billions of dollars every year due to extreme weather. Farmers need stronger structures to deal with unpredictable storms, heavy snowfall, and rising temperatures. Similarly, farmers face various other problems running their businesses because they lack the right structure.

Metal structures help agribusinesses deal with various difficulties effectively because these solutions are…

### Durable and Weather Resistant

#### Withstands Harsh Weather Conditions:

Since farming is done in open fields, farmers have to face extreme weather conditions like heavy rain, snowfall, high winds, and storms. Steel barn buildings are designed to withstand these adverse situations in the lives of farmers and ranchers. According to the National Weather Service, the U.S. experiences over 1,200 tornadoes annually, making durable structures essential for agribusinesses.

#### Fire Resistant Material:

Keeping crops and grain open in the field and under scorching sunlight can increase the risk of spoilage and fire hazards. Prefab metal barns are non-combustible. By choosing these structures, agribusinesses can reduce the risk of losing valuable crops, equipment, or livestock to accidental fires. This can be shocking, but according to a report from the National Fire Protection Association, nearly 326 agricultural fires occur yearly and sometimes cause millions in damages.

#### Pest and Rot Resistant:

Nowadays, farmers are investing in metal barns also because of their resistance to pests and rot, which is not present in wooden barns. They are the easy spaces for these little creatures to make a home, which increases the number of problems for you and your belongings. With steel barns, you and your business would not have to face this problem as steel barns are resistant to pests and rot. Likewise, steel doesn’t attract termites, rodents, and fungi.

### Economical Option and Requires Little Upkeep

#### Lower long-term maintenance costs:

Who would like to spend the most time repairing and replacing a structure? No one. When it comes to agribusinesses farmers want buildings that last for years with low maintenance. Steel barn structures do not need frequent repairs. You can clean them with one swipe, and they will turn out clean. But, it is missing when you choose wooden structures. They warp and rot and can’t be cleaned with one swipe.

#### Energy-Efficient Options:

According to the U.S. Department of Energy, properly insulated metal buildings can reduce energy costs by up to 50%. So, choosing an energy-efficient structure is a must for businesses as it helps them to reduce heating and cooling costs. They can save on electricity bills and use those savings for business growth. Steel barns can become energy-efficient with the help of insulation options like single bubble, double bubble, and woven R-17.

#### Fast and Affordable Installation

Rising competition in agribusiness requires faster turnaround times for building installation. Concrete and traditional structures take more time than steel buildings to be installed. Metal barns take less time to build because they are prefabricated. They can be assembled quickly, which reduces labor costs. Transportation of these lightweight components is also easy, again keeping the expenses low.

### Customizable and Easily Expandable

#### Flexible Designs:

You can customize [metal barn structures](https://www.vikingbarns.com/metal-barns) based on your farming, ranching, and personal needs. It is possible because of steel’s crucial characteristic, “Flexibility.” Since steel is highly flexible, you can customize barns to large open spaces, separate storage areas, stalls for livestock, and more. Moreover, you can easily change the layout if you think it does not meet your needs correctly.

#### Easily Expandable:

The size you choose for a barn can also meet your future needs? If you have a growth mindset, you will definitely need more storage spaces. In this situation, will you invest in a new structure again? If yes, it can be costly. Steel barns save you from indulging in unnecessary expenses as these structures are easily expandable as per need. This makes it easier to accommodate growing livestock numbers, new machinery, or additional storage needs without investing in a new building.

#### Multi-Purpose Use:

As you know, steel structures are flexible; they can be used for various purposes. They can serve multiple functions, from grain storage to equipment garages and livestock shelters. You can easily adapt the space based on seasonal demands.

Here are some examples of using a metal barn in different ways.

* Fertilizer Storage
* Commodity Storage
* Hay Storage
* Grain Storage
* Machinery/Equipment Storage
* Grain Storage
* Wheat Storage

### 4. Sustainable and Eco-friendly

#### Recycled endlessly:

Steel structures can be recycled endlessly without deteriorating quality. Well, steel is one of the most recyclable materials on the planet. According to the World Steel Association, 90% of structural steel is made from recycled content. The steel barn buildings can even last for at least 50 years with proper maintenance. Fifty years is a long time, which is good for the environment as till this period, you might not need new construction. And less construction means better sustainability.

#### Reduce deforestation:

Using metal barn buildings instead of wooden barns helps reduce the demand for timber. And there is no need to mention how crucial trees are. Wooden barns require wood, which can lead to deforestation. But with steel barn buildings, you can contribute to saving our planet because steel is highly recyclable, long-lasting, and durable.

# What are smart waterers and how do they benefit farm operations?

In the realm of modern agriculture, efficiency and sustainability are key drivers of innovation, leading to the adoption of advanced technologies designed to optimize resource use while maximizing productivity. Among these innovations, smart waterers stand out as a pivotal tool for enhancing farm operations. These sophisticated devices, integral to precision agriculture, leverage sensors and IoT (Internet of Things) connectivity to ensure that crops receive the exact amount of water needed at the right time. By doing so, smart waterers not only address the critical need for water conservation but also significantly influence the health and yield of crops.

The underlying technology of smart waterers allows for real-time monitoring and control of irrigation systems from a distance, using smartphones or computers. This means that farmers can automate watering schedules based on data-driven insights into soil moisture levels, weather forecasts, and plant requirements. The flexibility and precision that smart waterers provide help to eliminate the guesswork and manual labor traditionally associated with farm irrigation.

As water scarcity becomes an increasingly pressing concern globally, the importance of technologies like smart waterers that can minimize waste while enhancing crop growth cannot be overstated. These systems help ensure that water, a precious and often limited resource, is used as efficiently as possible. In addition to promoting water conservation, smart waterers also contribute to the reduction of energy use and the operational costs associated with manual or less sophisticated automated watering systems, making them a critical component in the future of sustainable agriculture.

## Overview of Smart Waterers Smart waterers, also known as intelligent watering systems or smart irrigation systems, are technology-driven tools designed to optimize water usage for agricultural purposes. These systems utilize sensors, data analytics, and automated hardware to ensure that crops receive the precise amount of water needed at the right times, improving efficiency and reducing water waste. The primary benefit of smart waterers is their ability to precisely manage irrigation based on real-time data. Traditional irrigation systems often operate on a set schedule and might water plants regardless of their actual needs or environmental conditions. In contrast, smart waterers can adjust to the soil moisture content, weather predictions, evaporation rates, and other critical factors. This targeted irrigation approach not only saves water but also promotes healthier crop growth and yield by avoiding over or under-watering. Efficient water management is increasingly critical in the face of global challenges such as water scarcity and climate change. By employing smart waterers, farms can significantly reduce the volume of water wasted during irrigation. This not only conservates an essential resource but also cuts costs related to water consumption. Furthermore, by maintaining optimal soil moisture levels, these systems help enhance nutrient uptake and reduce the stress on plants, which can lead to better overall farm productivity. Moreover, smart waterers integrate seamlessly into broader farm management systems, allowing for centralized control and monitoring of irrigation practices along with other farm activities. This connectivity not only simplizes management tasks but also provides valuable insights through data collection and analysis, enhancing decision-making processes. In summary, smart waterers represent a critical advancement in agricultural technology, offering numerous benefits to farming operations. They play a pivotal role in conserving water, enhancing crop health and yield, and simplifying farm management through integration with modern technologies. As water-related issues continue to gain importance globally, the adoption of such systems is likely to increase, driven by both environmental concerns and economic benefits.
